message:
perf(btree): make cell slot cache truly sparse

Fresh-eyes follow-up to cce92d4b: the cache no longer parses every cell on a miss, but it was still resizing the per-page slot vector to cell_count and filling it with None. Store only (cell_idx, slot) pairs so a single-cell miss does no page-wide placeholder work.

Adds an assertion that the cache contains one parsed slot after the first parse and two after the second parse.

Verification: cargo fmt --all --check; git diff --check; rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_beigemouse cargo test -p fsqlite-btree cell_slot_cache -- --nocapture; rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_beigemouse cargo check --workspace --all-targets; rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_beigemouse cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; ubs crates/fsqlite-btree/src/cursor.rs.
